reasoning: PKGBUILD safely downloads official binaries from trusted CDN with checksums. All reviewed files show standard IDE functionality with no malicious patterns. The inconclusive verdict stemmed from a technical error auditing a minified JS file, not a security risk.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen model failed to audit a minified JS file due to technical error (400 response), triggering inconclusive verdict. DeepSeek reviewed equivalent source files safely.
coverage_issues: Qwen skipped critical source files (e.g., googleVertexResolver.ts) but attempted minified dist/extension.js
Neither reviewed .install hooks or other build scripts (none exist in package)
learnings: Prioritize source files over minified/compiled code for readability
Add automatic fallback when encountering minified files: skip if source available
Improve error handling for large/minified files to avoid inconclusive verdicts
Explicitly flag non-build-time files (e.g., IDE extensions) as lower priority
